text.title: user-story-mapping Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for user-story-mapping. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Build the backbone first with verbs in user-journey order; then add tasks. Reverse order produces noise.
rule.rationale: Backbone-first approach ensures the context exists for meaningful task prioritization.

rule.statement: Walking skeleton is non-negotiable: exactly one task per backbone activity and end-to-end working flow.
rule.rationale: The skeleton is the minimum viable release contract; without it, Release 1 becomes a feature soup.

rule.statement: Each release slice must span the full backbone, not just one column. Otherwise users can't complete the journey.
rule.rationale: Partial-backbone releases are broken by definition.

rule.statement: Include error/edge tasks in the columns, not just happy path.
rule.rationale: Error handling determines user retention; happy path alone is incomplete.

rule.statement: Update the map at every quarterly review; treat it as living, not a deliverable.
rule.rationale: Maps drift; periodic sync with roadmap reality keeps them useful.

rule.statement: Pair the map with the spec: each release-bound task gets a story-card with acceptance criteria.
rule.rationale: Maps are a planning tool; acceptance criteria drive implementation and testing.

rule.statement: For solo dev work, drop the workshop ceremony but keep the structure: backbone + skeleton + 2 release slices.
rule.rationale: Structure applies even without cross-functional teams; it forces clarity on priority and scope.
